navigation_overview:
The top navigation bar appears on every page and contains the site name "MoodleTest" on the left, which links to the home page, followed by links for Home, Dashboard, and My Courses. On the right side, a notifications bell icon shows alerts when clicked, a messaging icon opens the messaging drawer, and the user's initials in a circular icon opens the user menu dropdown. The user menu contains links for Profile, Grades, Calendar, Private Files, Reports, and Preferences, followed by a Log out option that ends the session and returns to the login page. When viewing a course, a horizontal tab bar appears below the course title with tabs for Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, and Competencies. Teachers also see a Settings tab that is not visible to students. When viewing an activity, breadcrumbs at the top show the full navigation path from the course, with each segment as a clickable link. The Course Index sidebar on the left side of course pages provides a hierarchical tree of all sections and activities, with expand/collapse arrows per section and a close button (X) to hide the sidebar. The currently active item is highlighted.

nodes.title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser

module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can enter username and password to log in, access as a guest, and view cookie information; a lost-password link is present (disabled on test site).
Line 2: Displays Username and Password fields, Log in button, Access as a guest button, Cookies notice button, and inline error messages that clear the password on failed login.

Line 1: Users can view a personalized Dashboard, filter and search upcoming teaching actions, change timeline range and sort order, view the calendar, and create new events.
Line 2: Displays a personalized greeting, a Timeline block with time range, sort and search controls, and a Calendar monthly view with current month/year heading and course filter.

Line 1: Users can toggle Dashboard Edit mode to add blocks, and reset the page to default or cancel edits.
Line 2: Displays Edit mode controls including a Reset page to default button, a + Add a block button, and an Add a block page listing available block types (Comments, Course overview, Latest announcements, etc.).

Line 1: Users can browse and manage course cards, filter by status, search, sort, change layout, star a course, or remove it from view via the three-dot menu.
Line 2: Displays course cards showing banner image, clickable course name, category name, and controls above the grid for status, search, sort, and layout (Card, List, Summary).

Line 1: Users can navigate the course, expand or collapse sections, and open activities and resources from the course content view.
Line 2: Displays the course name as the heading, a navigation tab bar, collapsible sections with chevrons, and activities/resources listed with type icons and clickable names, plus a Collapse all link.

module_summaries.module_title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser
module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can enable Course Edit mode to rename, edit, duplicate, hide, move, delete sections and activities, perform bulk actions, and add activities or subsections via the Activity Chooser.
Line 2: Displays inline edit icons and three-dot menus for sections and activities, a bulk actions toolbar, + Add an activity or resource and + Add a subsection controls, and the Activity Chooser modal with category filters.

Line 1: Users can create an assignment by entering a required name, adding a description and files, and configuring availability, due and cut-off dates with enable toggles.
Line 2: Displays the required Assignment name field, Description rich text editor, Additional files upload area, and collapsible Availability panels with date/time pickers and Enable toggles for Allow submissions from, Due date, and Cut-off date.

Line 1: Users can edit course settings including full/short names, category, visibility, start/end dates, ID number, summary, image, format, and appearance options.
Line 2: Displays form fields and collapsible panels for Course full name, Course short name, Course category dropdown, Course visibility dropdown, start/end dates with Enable toggle, Course ID number, Course summary editor, Course image upload, and Course format/layout controls.

Line 1: Users can view and manage enrolled participants, open the enrol dialog, and build/apply complex filters or use alphabetical filters and bulk selection.
Line 2: Displays the enrolled-users scope dropdown, Enrol users button, filter builder (Any toggle, Select attribute dropdown, + Add condition), Clear/Apply filters buttons, alphabetical filters for First/Last name, and checkboxes for bulk selection.

Line 1: Users can view assignment details, open the grading interface for individual students, and navigate to assignment settings, submissions, and grading options.
Line 2: Displays Opened and Due dates, the full Description with attached files, a Grade button, a Grading summary panel (participants, submissions, Needs grading, Visibility, Time remaining), and a navigation tab bar.

Line 1: Users can search and filter student submissions, open student profiles, and preview or download submission files and online text.
Line 2: Displays a table of submissions with student identity (name, initials icon, profile link), submission status, grading status, submission date/time and time since submission, online text preview, and file submission links.

Line 1: Users can switch gradebook report types, search and filter students or groups, and perform per-column or per-cell grade actions.
Line 2: Displays a spreadsheet-style grade table with activities as columns and enrolled students as rows, a report-type selector, user search/filter controls, and per-column action menus.

Line 1: Users can message the teacher, edit their profile, and access privacy, course links, reports, and other related activity links from the profile page.
Line 2: Displays the teacher's circular initials icon, full name, Message button, optional profile description, and information cards for User details (email, visibility, timezone), Privacy and policies, Course details, Miscellaneous links, and Reports.

Line 1: Users can edit profile details, upload or change their picture, and expand or collapse sections (or use Expand all) while updating contact and personal information.
Line 2: Displays collapsible sections with General fields (First/Last name, Email and visibility, MoodleNet ID, City, Country, Timezone, Description), the user picture and upload area with description, and Additional names fields.

Line 1: Users can log out to terminate the authenticated session and be redirected to the login page.
Line 2: Displays a Log out control and enforces re-authentication to access protected pages after logout.

test_cases.title: Log out terminates session and redirects to login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and on any protected page
steps: Click the user's initials in the top navigation to open the user menu
Click "Log out"
test_cases.expected_result: The user session is terminated and the application redirects to the login page.

test_cases.title: Logout from user menu terminates session and shows login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in
steps: Click the user initials circular icon in the top navigation to open the user menu.
Click the Log out option in the user menu.
Observe the resulting page to confirm the session ended and the login page is displayed.
test_cases.expected_result: The session is terminated and the application displays the login page; authenticated content is no longer accessible without signing in again.

test_cases.title: Protected routes inaccessible after logout
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out after an active session
steps: After completing logout, attempt to navigate directly to a protected page (for example, Course Page or Dashboard) by entering its URL or clicking a saved link.
Verify the application prevents access to the protected content by redirecting to the login page or otherwise denying access.
test_cases.expected_result: Attempting to access a protected route after logout results in a redirect to the login page or an access denial; protected content is not shown.

test_cases.title: Browser refresh after logout does not restore authenticated session
test_cases.preconditions: User has just logged out
steps: Immediately after logging out, refresh the browser (reload the current page).
Confirm that the page remains on the login screen and no authenticated or user-specific content reappears.
test_cases.expected_result: Refreshing the browser after logout does not restore the authenticated session; the user remains logged out and only the login page or unauthenticated content is visible.

test_cases.title: Accessing a protected page after logout requires re-authentication
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and a protected page is open
steps: Click the user's initials in the top navigation to open the user menu
Click "Log out"
Use the browser Back button or attempt to load a protected page URL
test_cases.expected_result: The login page is shown and access to the protected page is denied until the user re-authenticates.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Access to all protected pages requires re-authentication after logout

test_cases.title: Logout in one tab prevents access to protected pages in other open tabs
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated with the application open in multiple browser tabs
steps: In one tab, click the user's initials in the top navigation to open the user menu and click "Log out"
Switch to another open tab with a protected page and attempt to interact with or refresh the page
test_cases.expected_result: The other tab requires the user to re-authenticate before accessing protected content.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Terminates the current authenticated session
